title: Agency Debt Securities - Federal Reserve Bank of New York

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: This source is an institutional informational page from the New York Fed describing its authority to buy and sell agency debt securities (Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, Federal Home Loan Banks) for the System Open Market Account (SOMA). The page explains that these transactions are conducted by the Open Market Trading Desk with primary dealers, that purchases inject reserve balances while sales or maturities drain them, and that the desk also provides limited transaction services to approximately 200 central banks and official institutions. Historical purchases occurred between 2009-2010 as part of large-scale asset purchase programs, with the Fed having first purchased agency debt in 1971 before ending purchases in the early 1990s. The page references reinvestment announcements dated October 29, 2025 and March 19, 2025.
key_points: The New York Fed's Open Market Trading Desk is authorized by the FOMC to buy and sell agency debt securities (Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, Federal Home Loan Banks) for the SOMA portfolio
Transactions are executed with primary dealers; purchases increase reserve balances while sales or maturities reduce them
Agency debt purchases between 2009-2010 were part of large-scale asset purchase programs aimed at lowering longer-term rates and supporting mortgage markets
The New York Fed provides transaction services to approximately 200 central banks, governments, and official institutions that lack in-house agency debt market capacity
The Fed first purchased agency debt in 1971, ended purchases in the early 1990s, and purchased agency discount notes briefly in 2008
Reinvestment announcements dated October 29, 2025 and March 19, 2025 are referenced but not detailed in this page
why_it_matters: This page provides essential context about the Federal Reserve's market operations infrastructure for agency debt, explaining the mechanics of how the Desk conducts purchases and sales and serves official sector counterparties. While it does not contain new policy decisions, it is a foundational reference for understanding Fed market structure and the SOMA portfolio management framework.
market_structure_context: The page describes the New York Fed's Open Market Trading Desk operations for agency debt securities, including execution with primary dealers, the impact on reserve balances, and services provided to approximately 200 official sector account holders. It references reinvestment policies for agency debt and agency MBS holdings as directed by the FOMC.
important_numbers: 200 central banks/governments served as official sector account holders
